RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 115-C, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Lisa Durnell, a Republican, proudly represents Howell County (District 154) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Durnell is a graduate of the University of Missouri, where she earned her Bachelor of Science degree in Agricultural Education in 1992. With over 32 years of experience as a certified residential appraiser, she has developed a deep understanding of local property markets. Most recently, Durnell served as the Deputy Collector for Howell County, where she managed property assessments and taxation efforts.
In addition to her legislative duties, Durnell is an active member of CrossWay Fellowship Church in Willow Springs and is a proud member of the United Federation of Republican Women. She is dedicated to serving her community, both in her professional life and through her civic involvement.
Durnell values family life and enjoys spending time with her husband, Dean, and their children, Emily and Ty. Whether it’s attending church, participating in local events, or engaging in community service, she is committed to making a positive impact on the lives of those around her.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to the selling of raw milk, raw milk products, or cream
Modifies guidelines for student participation in athletic contests organized by sex

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to gender transition procedures
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Modifies provisions relating to the placement of any surveillance or game camera on private property
Establishes protections for landowners taking wildlife on the landowner's property
Designates the "Lloyd Memorial Highway" in Newton County
Reestablishes the Second Amendment Preservation Act
Bans pesticides and pesticide ingredients that were made in China
Urges the United States Congress to reinstate mandatory country of origin labeling
Urges the United States Congress to reinstate mandatory country of origin labeling
